summary:
The available evidence does not clearly link pictorial warning labels to reductions in smoking-related health disparities among different demographic groups. Due to insufficient targeted studies evaluating equitable impacts, their effectiveness in addressing these disparities remains uncertain. 

(Note: This summary is based solely on Agent1’s stated reasoning.)

The current body of literature lacks specific findings connecting pictorial warning labels directly to reduced smoking-related health disparities by race, income, or region. Without robust comparative analyses focused on equity, it cannot be concluded that such labels effectively mitigate existing inequalities in smoke-related disease burden.  

(Only include content from Agent1; do NOT add external knowledge.) Summary: There is no clear evidence linking pictoral warning labels to reductions in smoking-related health disparities among different population groups. Their impact on narrowing inequities in smoking-related diseases remains unproven due to lacking dedicated equity-oriented study results.
